title: The founder chapter now rests on dated public releases, not a vague origin myth
summary: The strongest editorial move in the early company era is to treat February and August 2013 as the first durable public record. These documents make Sean Xiang legible inside a real enterprise-infrastructure narrative before later validation stacks up.
curatorNote: This exhibit matters because it converts the founding section from implied biography into documented market-facing language: co-founder and CEO, cloud privacy, virtualization, and data-at-rest trust.
lens: Best use: establish the first public company narrative without pretending to have exact incorporation-day paperwork.
Why it carries weight: both pieces are direct Bloombase URLs with dated release context and founder-positioning language.
Design role: document-style cards should feel archival, not promotional.

title: The middle years read best as a sequence of trust surfaces
summary: Rather than flooding the page with logos, the chronicle can show how compatibility, alliance status, security validation, and marketplace distribution accumulate across several infrastructure waves.
curatorNote: This is the chapter that proves durability. Each document marks a different kind of institutional trust: certification, alliance, security validation, and cloud distribution.
lens: Best use: demonstrate progression, not repetition.
Why it carries weight: the source mix is dense, first-party, and spread across multiple years rather than clustered in one burst.
Design role: the reader should feel an evidence cadence, not a partner wall.

title: The map becomes credible when expansion logic and operating footprint reinforce each other
summary: Vancouver matters because the 2018 Launch Academy profile explains why Canada enters the story, while the Bloombase offices page proves the expansion sits inside a wider operating footprint rather than a symbolic pin.
curatorNote: This exhibit protects the geography module from becoming decorative. It answers a real narrative question: how did the journey move across institutions and markets?
lens: Best use: tie place to strategic rationale, not motion for its own sake.
Why it carries weight: it combines a bridge profile with a direct company geography page.
Design role: editorial maps should orient the reader, then hand off to evidence.

title: The closing section has a credible present-tense sequence: reframing, visibility, then alliance execution
summary: The AI-era chapter is strongest when it shows how the story reopens: a 2023 technical reframing around NVIDIA DPUs and GPUs, ongoing public news visibility, and a 2025 alliance that explicitly names the AI era.
curatorNote: This is the best candidate for richer media treatment later because the sourcing is already direct enough to support stronger visual evidence without drifting into speculative futurism.
lens: Best use: argue relevance now, not nostalgia.
Why it carries weight: the chapter already has named, dated, first-party sources with a clear contemporary thesis.
Design role: future visuals here should behave like exhibits, not hero decoration.
